name: Denmark-Identity-Framework Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Technical debt → Systemic: Increased reliance on foreign suppliers → Strategic: Reduced digital sovereignty.
options: Mandate a national identity framework overhaul to include platform-neutral and fallback-capable access models.
Develop and certify an open-source, standards-based Danish eIDAS2 wallet replacement.
Implement a phased rollout of a new identity infrastructure leveraging blockchain technology for trust anchors.
review: Controls Platform Vendor Lock-in vs. Supplier Concentration Risk. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on existing business models and revenue streams.

name: Denmark-Procurement-Strategy Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Shift in procurement policies → Systemic: Increased transparency and competition → Strategic: Reduced reliance on foreign suppliers.
options: Influence AltID procurement to include platform-neutral and fallback-capable requirements.
Develop a set of open standards for digital identity that can be used by multiple vendors.
Create a public-private partnership to develop a new, platform-neutral eIDAS2 wallet.
review: Controls Procurement Neutrality vs. Supplier Concentration Risk. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential resistance from existing suppliers and their lobbying efforts.

name: Denmark-Regulatory-Framework Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Regulatory overhaul → Systemic: Increased compliance and security → Strategic: Enhanced digital sovereignty.
options: Advocate for changes to the Danish eIDAS2 wallet regulations to include platform-neutral and fallback-capable access models.
Develop a set of guidelines for the development of open standards for digital identity.
Create a new regulatory framework that prioritizes digital sovereignty and security.
review: Controls Regulatory Framework vs. Supplier Concentration Risk. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential bureaucratic hurdles and delays in implementing regulatory changes.

options: Develop a technical demonstrator for a platform-neutral eIDAS2 wallet.

review: Controls Technical Demonstrator vs. Supplier Concentration Risk. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential limitations and constraints of technical demonstrators.

review: Controls International Cooperation vs. Supplier Concentration Risk. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential challenges and complexities of international cooperation.

name: Denmark-Identity-Infrastructure-Leveraging Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Reduced reliance on Apple/Google → Systemic: Improved digital sovereignty → Strategic: Enhanced resilience and continuity of service
options: Develop open-source, platform-neutral mobile apps leveraging FIDO2/WebAuthn and OpenID Connect
Implement a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication path to reduce dependence on the mobile OS duopoly
Create a hybrid approach combining elements of both previous options for improved flexibility and security
review: Controls supplier concentration vs. platform neutrality. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of widespread adoption among Danish citizens.

name: Denmark-Identity-Framework Evolution Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased complexity → Systemic: Higher costs due to additional infrastructure requirements (30% increase) → Strategic: Reduced scalability and maintainability implications.
